title: ureadstringsyssetting
text: ureadstringsyssetting Read a string from the specified section of a specified ini file. Explain this code int ureadstringsyssetting (char*section, char*key, char*default, char*value, intmax, char*iniFile); Copy Parameters section Name of the section containing the specified key key Name of the key whose associated string is to be retrieved. default Default value to be copied to value if key is not found in the initialization file. This parameter cannot be NULL. value Requested value (Output) max Size of the buffer pointed to by value . iniFile Name of the initialization file. If this name is not fully qualified, Uniface uses the standard Windows search path for initialization files. Return Values The length of the string that was copied into value , not including the zero terminator. This function never returns an error. Use Microsoft Windows platforms only. Library: ulib Description This function is a wrapper for the Windows API function GetPrivateProfileString and follows the same rules. For extensive information about this function, visit the Microsoft DN technical documentation . Explain this code #include <h3gl.h> char val[200]; ureadstringsyssetting("mysection", "mykey", "foobar", val, sizeof(val)-1, ugetprofile()); Copy Related Links ugetprofile Return a pointer to the name of the current .ini file. uwritestringsyssetting Copies a string to a specified section of a specified ini file.
